description: Core Telegram Bot API domain abused by virtually all malware families using Telegram as C2 - HTTPS POST to /botTOKEN/sendMessage or /sendDocument endpoints
Telegram Bot API sendMessage endpoint - used by phishing kits and infostealers to exfiltrate credentials as text messages
Telegram Bot API sendDocument endpoint - used to upload stolen credential archives and screenshots to attacker chat
Telegram Bot API getUpdates endpoint - used by RATs (XWorm AsyncRAT) to poll for operator commands in real time
Telegram Bot API sendPhoto endpoint - used to exfiltrate screenshots captured by keyloggers and stealers
Telegram IP range AS62014 - all Telegram bot API traffic routes through these addresses - monitor/restrict in high-security environments
Telegram IP range AS62014 - Telegram API server subnet - used for all bot communications
Telegram IP range AS62014 - Telegram API server subnet
Telegram IP range AS62014 - Telegram API server subnet
Telegram IP range AS62014 - Telegram server subnet used for API calls
Agent Tesla C2 bot - display name bebetologxxz - exfiltrates to chat named 'Mr Grace 247' - identified via exposed API token in phishing kit JavaScript
Agent Tesla Telegram exfiltration bot - also received Styx Stealer reports - used by threat actor Mack_Sant aka Fucos - led to attribution of two operators
Styx Stealer Telegram C2 bot - operated via cobrasupports account - led to exposure of actor Sty1x location via leaked OPSEC data
PupkinStealer data exfiltration bot - bot ID 8013735771 - Russian-language bot name - developer alias Ardent
AccountDumpling Facebook phishing C2 bot (Bot-1) - display name VT張太海宣VT - approx 2900 victim records
AccountDumpling Facebook phishing C2 bot (Bot-2) - ROTATED/REVOKED - Unicode Fraktur display name - ~12000 victims
AccountDumpling replacement C2 bot (Bot-3) - replaced revoked Bot-2 - ~11000 victim records
AccountDumpling Blue Badge flow C2 bot (Bot-4) - Tich Xanh means blue checkmark in Vietnamese - ~4000 victims
Telegram operator handle with admin access to AccountDumpling credential exfiltration channels
ZeroTrace Team Telegram channel - malware development and distribution hub for Raven Stealer and Octalyn Stealer - created April 30 2025
#1 Telegram C2-using malware family - 77.7% of all Telegram C2 malware reports in 2024 - exfiltrates via /sendDocument API call to upload credential files
#2 Telegram C2 malware family by volume - .NET keylogger MaaS - exfiltrates credentials browser data screenshots via Telegram - VipKeylogger is a variant - sales site hxxps://snaketools.xyz
XWorm RAT - trojanized builder variant uses /sendDocument to exfiltrate passwords/cookies/IP info and /getUpdates for real-time operator commands via format /machine_id*command
AsyncRAT observed using Telegram as C2 channel - bidirectional command and control
Lumma Stealer (March 2025 update) retrieves actual C2 server address from Telegram channel name - channel name decoded via ROT13/ROT15 cipher - provides infrastructure resilience and evasion
Raven Stealer uses curl.exe to upload ZIP archive (username_RavenStealer.zip) via /sendDocument - targets browser passwords cookies payment info crypto wallets
Octalyn Stealer - shares structural similarities with Raven Stealer - same ZeroTrace Team developer - Telegram-based exfiltration
DeerStealer uses curl.exe POST to Telegram /sendMessage to send operator notifications at key execution stages - text in Russian (e.g. username–старт = start)
PupkinStealer uses Telegram as sole C2/exfil channel - .NET infostealer based on StormKitty codebase - developer alias Ardent
Open-source .NET infostealer - steals browser credentials cookies crypto wallet data - exfiltrates via Telegram - basis for PupkinStealer and other derivatives
Ghost Stealer MaaS - ~$20 single build / $100 lifetime - advertises Telegram as C2 (TG sender + no host needed) - rebranded to GhostLord in 2024 - developer alias GhostByte
Styx Stealer - Phemedrone Stealer fork - first appeared April 2024 - Telegram exfiltration - browser data crypto wallet credentials - operated by Turkey-based Sty1x
WSH RAT sends victim IP address geolocation city country and browser user-agent to Telegram C2 from the initial payload download page
Iranian Android RAT - uses Telegram Bot API for C2 only (not exfil) - steals SMS contacts Google accounts call history - commands received in Farsi
Iranian Android RAT - extends IRRAT - uses Telegram Bot API for both C2 and data exfiltration - polls getUpdates every 4.6 seconds for commands in Farsi

APT42 PowerShell backdoor - uses Telegram bot as C2 - any incoming Telegram message treated as executable PowerShell - AES-encrypted data exfiltration - first recorded APT42 Telegram C2 use - targets defense/government/NGOs
Russian-linked Golang backdoor using Telegram as C2 - fully functional despite appearing under development - C2 traffic indistinguishable from legitimate Telegram API use
VipKeylogger - variant of SnakeKeylogger - same Telegram exfiltration mechanism - emerged concurrent with decline of Agent Tesla (OriginLogger) in 2024
Process command line pattern - curl.exe calling Telegram sendDocument - used by Raven Stealer and DeerStealer - detectable via EDR process command line monitoring
PowerShell invoking Telegram API - common pattern across multiple malware families - monitor via Defender for Endpoint / Sentinel
WSH/VBScript process spawning connection to api.telegram.org - uncommon and suspicious in enterprise environments
All Telegram bot API traffic over HTTPS port 443 to Telegram AS62014 subnets - block outbound in environments where Telegram is not required
DNS/network block for api.telegram.org in environments where Telegram is not needed - prevents all Telegram-based malware C2 regardless of malware family

ioc_value: T1102.002 - Bidirectional Communication via Web Service
description: MITRE ATT&CK: Command and Control via Bidirectional Communication using Web Service (Telegram Bot API)

ioc_value: T1071.001 - Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols
description: MITRE ATT&CK: C2 via HTTPS to api.telegram.org - blends with normal web traffic - encrypted and difficult to inspect without TLS break-and-inspect

ioc_value: T1567.002 - Exfiltration to Cloud Storage / Service
description: MITRE ATT&CK: Exfiltration to a legitimate cloud communication service (Telegram) via Bot API
MITRE ATT&CK: PowerShell used to invoke Telegram Bot API calls for C2 and exfiltration
MITRE ATT&CK: cmd.exe / curl.exe used to make Telegram Bot API calls for exfiltration

ioc_value: DeviceNetworkEvents | where RemoteUrl contains "api.telegram.org" | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in ("Telegram.exe" "chrome.exe" "firefox.exe" "msedge.exe")
description: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint / Sentinel KQL: alert on any non-browser process making connections to api.telegram.org

ioc_value: DeviceProcessEvents | where ProcessCommandLine has "api.telegram.org" | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("powershell.exe" "cmd.exe" "wscript.exe" "curl.exe")
description: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint KQL: alert on command-line processes explicitly calling api.telegram.org - high-fidelity indicator of malicious Telegram C2

ioc_value: index=* (url="*api.telegram.org*" OR dest="api.telegram.org") NOT (process="Telegram.exe" OR process="chrome.exe" OR process="firefox.exe")
description: Splunk SPL: detect non-browser connections to api.telegram.org across network and endpoint telemetry
